edges.evidence_quote: Vincent Mangano also established the City Democratic Club, ostensibly to promote American values. In reality, the club was a cover for Murder, Inc., the notorious band of mainly Jewish hitmen who performed contract murders for the Cosa Nostra nationwide.
edges.context: The Mangano family (predecessor of Gambino) used Murder, Inc. as a cover for contract killings.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime activities in New York City
edges.context: The Gambino family is one of the Five Families.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino crime family ... is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the "Five Families" that dominate organized crime activities in New York City, within the nationwide criminal phenomenon known as the American Mafia.
edges.context: The Gambino family is part of the American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1916 the Camorra had assassinated Nicholas Morello, head of the Morello gang. In response, D'Aquila allied with the Morellos to fight the Camorra.
edges.context: D'Aquila allied with the Morello gang to fight the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1916 the Camorra had assassinated Nicholas Morello, head of the Morello gang. In response, D'Aquila allied with the Morellos to fight the Camorra.
edges.context: D'Aquila fought against the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1916 the Camorra had assassinated Nicholas Morello, head of the Morello gang.
edges.context: The Camorra assassinated the head of the Morello gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Soon after, D'Aquila absorbed the Mineo gang, making Mineo his first lieutenant.
edges.context: D'Aquila absorbed the Mineo gang.

edges.evidence_quote: By 1920, D'Aquila's only significant rival was Giuseppe "Joe the Boss" Masseria.
edges.context: D'Aquila and Masseria were rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: On October 10, 1928, Masseria gunmen assassinated Salvatore D'Aquila outside his home.
edges.context: Masseria's gunmen assassinated D'Aquila.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1930, the Castellammarese War started between Masseria and Salvatore Maranzano, the new leader of Cola Schirò's Castellammarese gang.
edges.context: Masseria fought against Maranzano's Castellammarese gang.

edges.evidence_quote: With the support of Frank Costello, boss of the Luciano crime family, the Commission confirmed Anastasia's ascension as boss of what was now the Anastasia crime family.
edges.context: The Luciano family supported Anastasia's ascension.

edges.evidence_quote: Gambino and Luciano then allegedly provided a part of the $100,000 paid to a Puerto Rican drug dealer to falsely implicate Genovese in a drug deal.
edges.context: Gambino conspired to frame Genovese.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1964, Joseph "Joe Bananas" Bonanno, the head of the Bonanno crime family, and Joseph Magliocco, the new boss of the Profaci crime family, conspired to kill Gambino and his allies on the Commission.
edges.context: Bonanno conspired to kill Gambino.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1964, Joseph "Joe Bananas" Bonanno, the head of the Bonanno crime family, and Joseph Magliocco, the new boss of the Profaci crime family, conspired to kill Gambino and his allies on the Commission.
edges.context: Profaci family boss Magliocco conspired to kill Gambino.

edges.context: Albert Anastasia, underboss of the Gambino family, led Murder, Inc.

edges.evidence_quote: took over his family in 1951—by all accounts, after murdering the family's founder Vincent Mangano—which was then recognized as the Anastasia crime family.
edges.context: The Gambino family was formerly known as the Anastasia crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Gotti could also count on the complicity of Gambino consigliere Joseph N. Gallo. ... he got the support of several important figures of his generation in the Lucchese, Colombo and Bonanno families.
edges.context: Gotti obtained support from the Lucchese family for his coup against Castellano.

edges.context: Genovese boss Gigante conspired to kill Gotti, making the families enemies.

edges.evidence_quote: Boško 'The Yugo' Radonjić, the head of the Westies in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan.
edges.context: Westies leader Radonjić helped Gotti by intimidating a witness.

edges.evidence_quote: Gigante conspired with Lucchese boss Anthony 'Tony Ducks' Corallo to have Gotti killed.
edges.context: Genovese and Lucchese bosses conspired together to kill Gotti.

edges.evidence_quote: Operation Old Bridge broke up a growing alliance between the Gambinos and the Sicilian Mafia, which wanted to get further into the drug trade.
edges.context: The Gambinos and Sicilian Mafia were forming an alliance for drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: He was allegedly the 'ambassador' in the US for the Inzerillo crime family.
edges.context: Frank Cali, a Gambino member, acted as ambassador for the Inzerillo family in the US.

edges.evidence_quote: On November 18, 2009, the NYPD arrested 22 members and associates of the Luchese and Gambino crime families as part of 'Operation Pure Luck'.
edges.context: Members of both families were arrested together in a joint operation, indicating cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2014, FBI and Italian police arrested 17 members and associates of the 'ndrangheta Mafia, in particular the Ursino clan, and 7 members and associates of the Gambino and Bonanno families.
edges.context: Gambino members were arrested alongside 'Ndrangheta members in a transatlantic drug ring.

edges.evidence_quote: US Attorney Loretta Lynch singled out Gambino family associate Franco Lupoi as the linchpin of the operation, accusing him of conspiring with his father-in-law, Nicola Antonio Simonetta, a member of the Ursino clan, to set up the network.
edges.context: A Gambino associate conspired with a member of the Ursino clan to set up a drug network.

edges.evidence_quote: Bonanno crime family soldier, Frank 'Frankie Boy' Salerno, was also arrested and accused of conspiring with the Gambino crime family.
edges.context: A Bonanno soldier conspired with the Gambino family in racketeering and drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: Among those arrested were ... Colombo family associate Charles Fusco.
edges.context: A Colombo associate was arrested alongside Gambino members in an illegal gambling and loansharking operation.

edges.evidence_quote: Gambino family capo Frank 'Calypso' Camuso, soldier Louis Astuto and associate Robert 'Rusty' Baselice were indicted along 20 other defendants including Genovese family soldier Christopher 'Jerry' Chierchio.
edges.context: Gambino and Genovese members were indicted together in a kickback scheme.

edges.context: Lucchese underboss ordered murder of Gambino member Bartholomew Boriello.

edges.context: Gambino soldier Joseph Francolino and Genovese capo Alphonse Malangone were convicted together for controlling carting industry.

edges.evidence_quote: Former soldier and major drug trafficker with known contacts in the Chicago Outfit.
edges.context: Gambino soldier Frank Pasqua had contacts with the Chicago Outfit for drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2016, he and 21 other members and associates of the Gambino, Bonanno and Genovese crime families were indicted as part of an illegal gambling and $15 million marijuana and oxycodone drug operation.
edges.context: Gambino, Bonanno, and Genovese members were indicted together for a joint drug and gambling operation.

edges.evidence_quote: It is noted he was involved with Genovese capo John Ardito contracting and subcontracting projects in the Bronx and Westchester area.
edges.context: A Gambino capo worked with a Genovese capo on construction projects, indicating cooperation between the families.

edges.evidence_quote: Its most prominent member during this time was its underboss Albert Anastasia, who rose to infamy as the operating head of the underworld's enforcement arm, Murder, Inc.
edges.context: Albert Anastasia, underboss of the Gambino family, was the head of Murder, Inc.

edges.evidence_quote: He became a bootlegger and joined the D'Aquila gang during the 1920s – later evolved into the Gambino crime family.
edges.context: The D'Aquila gang later evolved into the Gambino crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Bonanno crime family hitmen Vincent "Kojak" Giattino and Thomas Pitera murdered Johnson as a favor for Gotti
edges.context: Bonanno hitmen murdered a Gambino associate as a favor for Gambino boss John Gotti.

edges.evidence_quote: His brother was shot to death in 1981 in a dispute related with the Gambino and Colombo crime families
edges.context: A dispute involving both Gambino and Colombo families led to a murder.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1989, he helped arrange the murder of publisher and sanitation business owner Fred Weiss, who was shot to death by the New Jersey DeCavalcante crime family as a favor to John J. Gotti.
edges.context: DeCavalcante family murdered Fred Weiss as a favor to Gambino boss John Gotti.

edges.evidence_quote: The family has also cooperated with the Genovese family in New Haven County.
edges.context: Gambino family cooperated with Genovese family in New Haven County.

edges.evidence_quote: Fappiano admitted to corruption, bribery and the extortion of construction companies based in Manhattan and Brooklyn, with the Genovese crime family.
edges.context: Gambino soldier Fappiano engaged in extortion with the Genovese family.

edges.evidence_quote: Carlo Gambino created an alliance between the Gambino family and three Sicilian clans: the Inzerillos, the Spatolas and the Di Maggios.
edges.context: Carlo Gambino formed an alliance with the Inzerillo clan.

edges.context: Cherry Hill Gambinos is a Sicilian faction of the Gambino family.

edges.evidence_quote: News reports in July 2019 indicated that a recent police investigation confirmed strong links between the Palermo area Cosa Nostra and the Gambino crime family in New York.
edges.context: Police investigation confirmed strong links between Palermo Cosa Nostra and Gambino family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Lucchese-Genovese alliance (1953–1985) between Carlo Gambino, Tommy Lucchese, and Vito Genovese began with a plot to take over the Mafia Commission by murdering family bosses Frank Costello and Albert Anastasia.
edges.context: Gambino and Lucchese families were allied from 1953 to 1985, cooperating in Commission plots and rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Lucchese alliance (1999–present) was initiated by acting Luchese boss Steven Crea in 1999.
edges.context: Renewed alliance between Gambino and Lucchese families from 1999 onward, cooperating in construction extortion and gambling.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Lucchese-Genovese alliance (1953–1985) between Carlo Gambino, Tommy Lucchese, and Vito Genovese began with a plot to take over the Mafia Commission by murdering family bosses Frank Costello and Albert Anastasia.
edges.context: Gambino and Genovese were initially allied in a plot to take over the Commission, but later Gambino conspired to remove Genovese.

edges.evidence_quote: After assuming power, Gambino started conspiring with Lucchese to remove their former ally Genovese.
edges.context: Gambino conspired with Lucchese to remove Genovese, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Bonanno alliance (1991–2004) started with John Gotti and new Bonanno boss Joseph Massino.
edges.context: Gambino and Bonanno families were allied from 1991 to 2004, cooperating on Commission matters and traditional crimes.

edges.evidence_quote: The Gambino-Westies alliance (1977–1992) resulted from an ongoing war between the Genovese family and the Westies.
edges.context: Gambino family brokered a peace and formed an alliance with the Westies from 1977 to 1992.

edges.evidence_quote: John Gotti of the Gambino family had sent one of their explosives experts to Sicily to work with the Corleonesi.
edges.context: Gambino family cooperated with Corleonesi by sending an explosives expert to help plan a bombing.
